title: Student Records — Structured Data
description: Define a Student struct with fields: name (string), grades (double array), average (double), and letterGrade (char). Replace the parallel arrays with an array of Student structs. Access members using the dot operator.
test_code:
assert 'struct' in cpp, 'Must define a struct'
assert 'Student' in cpp, 'Struct should be named Student'
cpp_after_struct = cpp[cpp.index('struct'):] if 'struct' in cpp else cpp
assert '.name' in cpp or '.average' in cpp or '.letterGrade' in cpp or '.grades' in cpp, 'Must access struct members using the dot operator (.)'
assert 'Student' in cpp.split('main')[1] if 'main' in cpp else True, 'Must use the Student struct in main'
assert cpp.count('.') >= 3, 'Should use dot operator multiple times to access struct members'
acceptance_criteria: Defines a Student struct with name, grades, average, and letterGrade fields
Uses an array of Student structs instead of parallel arrays
Accesses struct members using the dot operator
Functions work with the Student struct
hints: Define the struct above main: struct Student { string name; double grades[NUM_GRADES]; double average; char letterGrade; };
Declare an array of structs: Student roster[MAX_STUDENTS];
You can pass a Student to functions: void displayStudent(Student s) { ... }
